content.content:
1→# CLAUDE.md
     2→
     3→This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
     4→
     5→## Project Overview
     6→
     7→Maimai Semi-Automated Recruitment Assistant - A Python CLI tool for semi-automating candidate search, profile extraction, and outreach on Maimai (脉脉), China's professional networking platform. Core modules (browser, auth, search, profile, message, export) are implemented and functional.
     8→
     9→## Build & Development Commands
    10→
    11→```bash
    12→# Install dependencies
    13→pip install -r requirements.txt
    14→patchright install chrome   # Uses real Chrome for better anti-detection
    15→# Or: patchright install chromium
    16→
    17→# Initialize database (creates SQLite tables)
    18→python scripts/setup_db.py
    19→
    20→# Run CLI
    21→python src/cli/main.py --help
    22→python src/cli/main.py login                    # Manual login (saves session)
    23→python src/cli/main.py search -k "前端工程师" -p 2  # Search candidates
    24→python src/cli/main.py search -k "前端" -k "后端" -p 2  # Multi-keyword search
    25→python src/cli/main.py search -k "前端" -c "字节" -c "快手" -p 2  # Multi-company filter
    26→python src/cli/main.py profile <maimai_id>       # View profile
    27→python src/cli/main.py message <id> -t greeting  # Send message
    28→python src/cli/main.py export -o out.xlsx        # Export data
    29→python src/cli/main.py pipeline -k "前端" -t greeting -v "recruiter_name=张三" -p 2  # Search + batch message
    30→python src/cli/main.py pipeline -k "前端" -k "后端" -t greeting --dry-run  # Pipeline preview only
    31→python src/cli/main.py pipeline -k "研发" -c "亚马逊" -c "快手" -t greeting -p 3  # Multi-company filter
    32→python src/cli/main.py pipeline -k "前端" -t greeting -p 10 -b 2  # Batch: search 2 pages, send, repeat
    33→python src/cli/main.py pipeline --resume                         # Resume interrupted pipeline
    34→python src/cli/main.py templates                 # List templates
    35→python src/cli/main.py status                    # Rate limit status
    36→python src/cli/main.py logout                    # Clear session
    37→
    38→# Testing
    39→pytest                      # Run all tests
    40→pytest --cov=src tests/    # With coverage
    41→
    42→# Code quality
    43→black src/ tests/          # Format code
    44→ruff check src/ tests/     # Lint
    45→mypy src/                  # Type check
    46→```
    47→
    48→## Architecture
    49→
    50→```
    51→CLI Layer (Click)           →  src/cli/main.py (10 commands: login, search, profile, message, pipeline, export, templates, status, logout)
    52→       ↓
    53→Feature Modules             →  src/modules/ (auth, search, profile, message, export)
    54→       ↓
    55→Core Infrastructure         →  src/core/ (browser, session, anti_detection)
    56→       ↓
    57→┌──────┴──────┬────────────┐
    58→Database      Config       Utilities
    59→(SQLAlchemy)  (3 JSON)     (logging, validation, rate limiting)
    60→```
    61→
    62→**Core infrastructure** (in `src/core/`):
    63→- `browser.py` - Singleton BrowserManager with Patchright (real Chrome via `channel="chrome"`), persistent context, lazy asyncio.Lock
    64→- `session.py` - SessionManager for save/load cookies via Patchright storage_state
    65→- `anti_detection.py` - Multi-layer anti-detection: Patchright (undetectable automation), Emunium (human-like click/type), Ghost Cursor algorithm (Fitts's Law + cubic Bezier mouse trajectories with sigmoid easing and micro-overshoot), stealth JS, Chrome 131-136 UA strings
    66→
    67→**Feature modules** (in `src/modules/`):
    68→- `auth.py` - Manual login flow with session detection, captcha detection/pause
    69→- `search.py` - Candidate search with JS-based extraction (name+company+title hash IDs, location, profile URL), multi-keyword support, multi-company filtering, pagination, captcha detection with adaptive throttle reporting
    70→- `profile.py` - Profile extraction (education, work experience, skills), async rate limiting
    71→- `message.py` - Template rendering with variable substitution, questionary-based confirmation (unsafe_ask for async compatibility), batch sending with single rate-limit check, auto-creates Candidate records for FK integrity, message send verification, existing conversation detection
    72→- `export.py` - Dual-format export (Excel with openpyxl formatting / CSV)
    73→
    74→**Utilities** (in `src/utils/`):
    75→- `config.py` - Singleton config loader with dot-notation access (e.g., `config.get('browser.headless')`)
    76→- `rate_limiter.py` - Database-backed rate limiting with configurable limits from `settings.json`, adaptive throttling (auto-reduces limits on captcha/error detection)
    77→- `delays.py` - Human-like delays using log-normal distribution, fatigue simulation (delays increase over session), random distraction pauses, time-of-day awareness
    78→- `retry.py` - Navigation retry with tenacity (3 attempts, exponential backoff) for `page.goto()` calls
    79→- `checkpoint.py` - Pipeline checkpoint save/load for resume capability (`--resume` flag)
    80→- `scoring.py` - Candidate scoring by title seniority, company tier, and keyword relevance for priority-based messaging
    81→- `validators.py` - Input validation for IDs, keywords, URLs, file paths
    82→- `logger.py` - Loguru-based logging with rotation
    83→
    84→**Database** (`src/models/database.py`): SQLAlchemy ORM with 4 tables - Candidates, Messages, SearchHistory, ActionLogs. `DatabaseManager.session_scope()` context manager for safe session handling (auto commit/rollback/close).
    85→
    86→## Configuration Files
    87→
    88→- `config/settings.json` - Runtime settings (browser, delays, paths)
    89→- `config/selectors.json` - CSS selectors for Maimai pages (**PLACEHOLDERS - must be updated with actual selectors**)
    90→- `config/message_templates.json` - 5 message templates with variable substitution
    91→
    92→## Key Constraints
    93→
    94→- **Rate limits are configurable** via `config/settings.json` with adaptive throttling (auto-reduces on captcha/error)
    95→- **Selectors updated** - JS-based extraction uses avatar image detection rather than CSS selectors; `selectors.json` has real Maimai selectors but primary extraction is via `page.evaluate()`
    96→- **Python 3.10+** required
    97→- All message operations require human confirmation
    98→- **Captcha handling** - search and login flows auto-detect captcha elements and pause for manual resolution
    99→- **Navigation retries** - all `page.goto()` calls go through `retry_navigation()` (tenacity, 3 attempts, exponential backoff)
   100→- **DB sessions** - use `db_manager.session_scope()` context manager for all DB operations (auto commit/rollback/close); avoid manual `get_session()` / `commit()` / `close()` patterns
   101→- **asyncio** - `run_async()` uses `asyncio.run()`; within async code use `asyncio.get_running_loop()` (never `get_event_loop()`)
   102→- **Search IDs** - generated from `hash(name + company + title)`, not name alone; DB dedup also checks name+company
   103→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

From Todos to Tasks: How Claude Code’s… | by Rick Hightower | Jan, 2026 | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/@richardhightower/claude-code-todos-to-tasks-5a1b0e351a1c"},{"title":"Best practices for Claude Code subagents","url":"https://www.pubnub.com/blog/best-practices-for-claude-code-sub-agents/"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Claude Code subagent Task tool configuration for 2026:

## Overview

Subagents are specialized AI assistants that handle specific types of tasks. Each subagent runs in its own context window with a custom system prompt, specific tool access, and independent permissions.

## Task Tool Parameters

The Task tool uses parameters including subagent_type, description, run_in_background, and prompt to configure subagent execution.

Key configuration parameters include:
- **subagent_type**: Such as "general-purpose" or "Plan"
- **description**: A short 3-5 word summary of the task
- **prompt**: Detailed instructions for the subagent
- **run_in_background**: Boolean to run tasks concurrently
- **model**: Optional parameter to specify which Claude model to use (e.g., "haiku" for faster/cheaper execution)

## Tool Permissions

The allowed_tools parameter restricts what the agent can do, with syntax like "Bash(cargo *)" meaning the agent can run cargo commands but not arbitrary shell commands.(no content)Each agent has minimal necessary permissions - read-only agents use Read, Grep, Glob; research agents use Read, Grep, Glob, WebFetch, WebSearch; code writers use Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Glob, Grep.

## Parallel Execution

The killer feature of Claude Tasks is parallel execution. You can spawn multiple sub-agents, each with scoped access to specific directories, working on independent features simultaneously.(no content)The Task tool must be included in allowedTools since Claude invokes subagents through the Task tool. Subagents cannot spawn their own subagents - don't include Task in a subagent's tools array.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text:
Based on the search results, here's a comprehensive guide on setting up sub-agents in Claude Code:

## Sub-Agents Overview

Sub-agents are specialized AI assistants that run in isolated contexts with custom system prompts, specific tool access, and independent permissions. They enable parallel task execution and task orchestration.

## Setting Up Sub-Agents with the Task Tool

Sub-agents are created using the **Task tool** with the following key parameters:

### Core Parameters

- **subagent_type**: Specifies the type of sub-agent (e.g., "general-purpose", "Plan")
- **description**: A concise 3-5 word summary of the task
- **prompt**: Detailed instructions for the sub-agent to follow
- **run_in_background**: Boolean flag to execute tasks concurrently/in parallel
- **model**: Optional parameter to specify which Claude model (e.g., "haiku" for faster/cheaper execution)

### Tool Permissions

Each sub-agent gets scoped tool access via the `allowed_tools` parameter:
- **Read-only agents**: Read, Grep, Glob
- **Research agents**: Read, Grep, Glob, WebFetch, WebSearch
- **Code writers**: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Glob, Grep

**Important**: The Task tool itself must be included in your main permissions, but sub-agents cannot spawn their own sub-agents (do not include Task in a subagent's tools array).

## Parallel Execution Pattern

The key feature of sub-agents is parallel execution. You can spawn multiple sub-agents working on independent features simultaneously:

```python
# Pseudo-example of parallel task pattern
Task(
    subagent_type="general-purpose",
    description="Feature A implementation",
    prompt="Implement feature A...",
    run_in_background=True
)

Task(
    subagent_type="general-purpose", 
    description="Feature B implementation",
    prompt="Implement feature B...",
    run_in_background=True
)
# Both execute in parallel
```

## Claude Agent SDK

Beyond Claude Code's Task tool, Anthropic also provides the **Claude Agent SDK** (available for Node.js/TypeScript and Python) for building custom AI agents. This is useful if you need:
- More control over agent behavior and tool access
- Custom business logic and integrations
- Hosting custom agents as services

The SDK integrates with MCP (Model Context Protocol) for remote tool connections and supports session management, permissions, and cost tracking.

## In Your Project

Looking at your current setup, you have the `superpowers` plugin which includes several agent-related skills like `/superpowers:subagent-driven-development`. This suggests you can use Claude Code's task orchestration for parallel execution of independent implementation tasks.

Sources:
- [Create custom subagents - Claude Code Docs](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents)
- [The Task Tool: Claude Code's Agent Orchestration System](https://dev.to/bhaidar/the-task-tool-claude-codes-agent-orchestration-system-4bf2)
- [ClaudeLog - Task/Agent Tools](https://claudelog.com/mechanics/task-agent-tools/)
- [Subagents in the SDK - Claude API Docs](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/subagents)
- [Best practices for Claude Code subagents](https://www.pubnub.com/blog/best-practices-for-claude-code-sub-agents/)
